title: Hotel Hopper
skill: Places, signs, and practical exam vocabulary
whyItMatters: The paper asks Mihika to identify where to go or what a place is for. She needs quick recognition, not slow translation.
mnemonic: Reception receives. Restaurant restores hunger. Garage guards cars.
source: Practice Paper II: sign and location recognition

demo.prompt: Tu peux garer ta voiture ici.
demo.explanation: The clue is the action. Garer means to park, so the matching place is the garage.

title: Verb Workshop
whyItMatters: Mihika sees present tense in gap fills before she converts those same verbs into passé composé.
mnemonic: Present tense is the toolbox. Passé composé is the same toolbox with a helper verb attached.
source: Practice Paper II: prendre, mettre, remplir, partir, pouvoir

demo.explanation: With vous in the present tense, partir becomes partez.

title: Time Travel Lab
whyItMatters: Most verbs in the uploaded practice sets use avoir, so Mihika needs this as the default pathway.
mnemonic: Avoir is the backpack helper: I carry ai, as, a, avons, avez, ont into the past.

demo.answer: Ma mère a perdu ses clés.
demo.explanation: Perdre normally takes avoir in the passé composé, so we use a + perdu.

title: Elevator of Movement
whyItMatters: The worksheets strongly focus on movement and state-change verbs such as aller, arriver, partir, entrer, naître, mourir and rester.
mnemonic: If the subject moved or changed state, ask: did it ride the être elevator? Then make the ending agree.

demo.prompt: Elle ______ au cinéma avec ses amis. [aller]
demo.answer: Elle est allée au cinéma avec ses amis.
demo.explanation: Aller takes être. Because the subject is feminine singular, the past participle becomes allée.

title: Grammar Repair Station
whyItMatters: The correction worksheet trains Mihika to notice whether the helper verb, participle, and agreement are all correct.
mnemonic: Repair in three taps: helper, participle, agreement.

demo.explanation: Aller is an être verb, not an avoir verb. With nous, the participle also takes s: allés.

title: Story Scout
skill: Reading comprehension and short guided writing
whyItMatters: The paper ends with a reading task and a guided piece titled Ma sortie récente, so Mihika needs both recall and composition confidence.
mnemonic: Read for gifts, floors, and reasons. Write with who, where, what, wore, ate.
source: Practice Paper II: shopping text and Ma sortie récente

demo.prompt: Pourquoi M. Lafont fait-il du shopping aujourd'hui ?
demo.answer: Parce que c'est Noël ce weekend et il achète pour sa famille.
demo.explanation: The reason appears at the start of the passage.

title: Morning & Evening
whyItMatters: Tricolore 3C tests reflexive verbs — se laver, se lever, se coucher, s'habiller, se réveiller. These have an extra pronoun that changes with the subject.
mnemonic: Reflexive = the action bounces back. Je ME lève, tu TE lèves, il SE lève. The pronoun mirrors the subject.

demo.prompt: Le matin, je ______ à sept heures. [se lever]
demo.answer: Je me lève à sept heures.
demo.explanation: Se lever with je becomes 'me lève'. The reflexive pronoun me goes before the verb.

title: Mais Non!
whyItMatters: Tricolore 3D/3E combines two tricky patterns: negating reflexive verbs (ne...pas wraps around the pronoun+verb) and using vouloir + infinitive.
mnemonic: NE wraps the reflexive sandwich: je NE me lève PAS. Vouloir wants an infinitive friend: je veux JOUER.
source: Tricolore 2, Units 3D-3E: Comment ça se passe / C'est mercredi

demo.prompt: Write the negative: Elle se baigne.
demo.explanation: In the negative, ne goes before the reflexive pronoun (se) and pas goes after the verb (baigne).

title: C'est Moi!
whyItMatters: Tricolore 4A tests physical descriptions (height, hair, eyes), clothing vocabulary, and greeting/introduction phrases with tu and vous.
mnemonic: Height first (je suis grand/petit), then hair (j'ai les cheveux...), then eyes (j'ai les yeux...), then clothes (je porte...).

demo.prompt: Describe André: average height, short brown hair, wearing a tracksuit and blue t-shirt.
demo.answer: Il est de taille moyenne. Il a les cheveux courts et bruns. Il porte un jogging et un tee-shirt bleu.
demo.explanation: Use être for height, avoir for hair/eyes, porter for clothes.

title: Yesterday's News
whyItMatters: Tricolore 4C introduces passé composé with avoir and teaches time expressions like hier, ce matin, la semaine dernière. This is where Mihika first learns to talk about the past.
mnemonic: Two verbs make the past: AVOIR in the present + past participle. Hier j'AI MANGÉ = Yesterday I ate.

demo.prompt: Translate to French: Did you buy something?
demo.explanation: Passé composé: tu (subject) + as (avoir) + acheté (past participle of acheter).

title: Party Time!
skill: Perfect tense of -er verbs and recent activities
whyItMatters: Tricolore 4D drills the -er verb pattern specifically: remove -er, add -é. This covers most common French verbs (manger, jouer, regarder, écouter, danser, acheter, porter).
mnemonic: The -er → -é rule: chop the R, add the accent. Manger → mangé. Jouer → joué. Danser → dansé.

demo.prompt: Complete with the past participle: Nous avons ______ de la pizza. [manger]
demo.answer: Nous avons mangé de la pizza.
demo.explanation: Manger is an -er verb. Remove -er, add -é: mangé.
